about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Kiwashi Technology has two business divisions
about.softwareDesc: We focus on developing process automation systems to help enterprises transform repetitive, time-consuming, and error-prone workflows into stable and traceable digital systems, reducing manual operation costs and improving overall operational efficiency. In many organizations, workflows often rely on manual operations and individual experience. As the scale grows, problems also expand. We gain a deep understanding of actual workflows, reorganize the data flow and decision points at each stage, and provide customized automation systems that meet practical needs. In system design, we emphasize scalability and long-term maintainability, ensuring systems can adjust as the organization grows without frequent reconstruction. At the same time, we emphasize process transparency, allowing managers to grasp status in real-time, reduce communication costs, and improve overall decision-making efficiency.

services.title: Products lead the direction, collaboration supports the rollout
services.subtitle: Our long-term direction is not to take every disconnected project, but to deepen our products first and extend those proven capabilities into the right kinds of partnerships.
services.highlightLead: This section should not frame us like a generic outsourcing studio. It should make it clear that products are the core, and collaboration exists mainly for needs that align with our product direction.
services.signal1: Product capabilities keep compounding instead of being rebuilt from scratch each time
services.signal2: We support enterprise adoption, product extensions, and internal integrations
services.signal3: Collaboration projects are prioritized when they fit our core architecture
services.productTitle: Our main focus is concentrated on two product tracks
services.productDescription: We invest our core technical energy into products that can keep evolving over time, rather than spreading resources evenly across every kind of commissioned work.
services.product1Desc: Built for repetitive and rules-based computer work, helping companies gradually turn manual operating steps into system-driven execution.
services.product2Desc: Unifies management, data, permissions, and internal workflows into one stable system so teams can operate with less fragmentation.
services.supportTitle: When needs align with the product direction, we also build with partners
services.supportDescription: We still take on collaboration work, but mainly around adoption, extension, and integration instead of accepting every type of custom project. That keeps delivery quality aligned with product growth.
services.support1Desc: Start from the existing product foundation and add the modules a business actually needs, instead of rebuilding everything from zero.
services.support2Desc: Connect product capabilities into existing tools, data sources, and operating routines to reduce adoption friction.
services.support3Desc: Provide continued support around stability, permissions, performance, and workflow refinement after launch.

productsPage.title: Building enterprise systems that truly work in practice
productsPage.subtitle: Our current product focus is built around two core directions: workflow automation systems and internal company systems. One is designed to replace repetitive computer-based work at scale, and the other helps companies consolidate management, data, and permissions into a stable internal platform.
productsPage.panelText: This page is not just a feature summary. It is meant to help visitors quickly understand what kind of work can be automated, what kind of internal management can be systemized, and how these products change daily operations after rollout.
productsPage.highlight1: Workflow automation can replace large amounts of repetitive computer work
productsPage.highlight2: Internal systems can be tailored around department flow and permission logic
productsPage.highlight3: Both products are designed around stability, scalability, and traceability
productsPage.flow1: Map existing manual and computer-based workflows
productsPage.flow2: Convert them into executable and traceable system modules
productsPage.flow3: Maintain and expand them as operations grow
productsPage.detailTitle: We do not just build interfaces. We turn processes into system capability.
productsPage.detailText: Whether it is automation or an internal system, our product planning starts from real business operation. The goal is to cut manual time, reduce error rates, and leave enough room for future expansion.
productsPage.principle1: We first identify which work still depends on repeated human computer operation, then prioritize automation there.
productsPage.principle2: Internal systems are designed around permissions, data integration, and process visibility, not just visual polish.
productsPage.principle3: Productization should create a stable core while still allowing companies to extend around their real operating model.

product1.name: Workflow Automation System
product1.description: Built for operational tasks that still rely on repeated computer actions. From data entry and comparison to step-by-step execution, the system is designed to remove heavy manual dependence.
product1.audience: Administrative, operational, and workflow-heavy teams with repeated computer tasks
product1.value: Turns step-based human work into stable automated execution.
product1.point1: Replaces large amounts of repetitive computer operation
product1.point2: Cuts manual errors and shortens execution time
product1.point3: Automation rules can be tailored to actual business flow

product2.name: Internal Company System
product2.description: A dedicated internal platform tailored to company management needs, bringing data, process, permissions, and daily operations into one stable system structure.
product2.audience: Organizations that need department data management, workflow standardization, and internal visibility
product2.value: Creates clearer internal flow, stronger permission control, and more sustainable data continuity.
product2.point1: Can integrate forms, backend tools, data lookup, and role management
product2.point2: System structure can be tailored to the company’s organization and workflow
product2.point3: Designed with maintenance, expansion, and version upgrades in mind
